claims.quote: You must volunteer for a minimum of 100 hours. Most volunteers come in for one 4 hours shift each week, so you would reach 100 hours in six months.

claims.claimText: Accepted volunteers undergo drug screening and TB test paid by AdventHealth.

claims.quote: Prospective volunteers must provide proof of vaccinations for Mumps, Measles, Rubella, and Chicken Pox.
claims.claimText: Volunteers must show proof of MMR and varicella vaccinations.

claims.quote: You will need to bring two (2) letters of recommendation with you to that interview.
claims.claimText: Two letters of recommendation required at the volunteer interview.

futureLaneSignals.quote:
Visa Sponsorship: H-1B Specialty Occupation
H-1B visas are authorized through USCIS and permits temporary employment in the U.S. for foreign nationals who intend to work in a specialty occupation. To qualify as an H-1B holding Medical Resident you MUST pass all three steps of Medical Licensing Examination (USLME) prior to AdventHealth filing your H1B petition.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: AdventHealth sponsors H-1B specialty occupation visas for medical residents who have passed all three USMLE steps prior to petition filing.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: H-1B sponsorship for medical residents is a residency-stage trainee benefit.

futureLaneSignals.quote: H-1B visas are valid for up to three years, with the option to extend for another three (totaling 6 years). H-1B visa holders can obtain permanent residency (green card) through AdventHealth once an offer of future employment is established.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: H-1B at AdventHealth is valid up to 3 years (extendable to 6) and may lead to green card sponsorship upon a future employment offer.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Green card sponsorship tied to a future employment offer falls into attending/post-trainee career stage.
